title: TCS NQT Mock Test 1
description: Simulates the TCS National Qualifier Test pattern: Verbal Ability, Reasoning, and Quantitative Aptitude sections.
questions: In a class, 40% of students play cricket, 30% play football, and 20% play both. What percentage play neither?
A train 150 metres long is running at a speed of 60 km/h. How long will it take to cross a pole?
A shopkeeper bought an article for Rs. 450 and sold it for Rs. 540. What is his profit percentage?
What is the compound interest on Rs. 8000 at 10% per annum for 2 years, compounded annually?
A can complete a piece of work in 12 days and B can complete the same work in 18 days. If they work together, in how many days will the work be completed?
If A:B = 3:4 and B:C = 4:5, find A:B:C.
What is the smallest number that should be added to 1000 to make it exactly divisible by 13?
The average of five consecutive odd numbers is 35. What is the largest number?
In how many ways can the letters of the word 'LEADER' be arranged?
A bag contains 4 red, 5 blue, and 3 green balls. What is the probability of picking a red ball?
Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the daughter of the only son of my grandfather.' How is the woman in the photograph related to the man?
In a row of students, Rahul is 15th from the left and 20th from the right. How many students are in the row?
What comes next in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
If APPLE is coded as 12234, how is PLEA coded?
All roses are flowers. Some flowers fade quickly. Which conclusion follows?
typeof null
What does Array.prototype.map() return?
Which HTTP method is conventionally used to update an existing resource entirely (replacing it)?
What HTTP status code should be returned when a resource is successfully created via a POST request?
What is the main advantage of using JWT (JSON Web Tokens) over traditional session-based authentication?
